description: Lodestone outbound network allowlist. Lists EVERY URL pattern any Lodestone build, install, or runtime path is permitted to contact. Anything not on this list landing in compiled dist/ fails the §18 build-artifact privacy audit (packages/shared/src/net/__tests__/no-outbound-urls.test.ts) and the CI workflow's privacy-audit step (.github/workflows/ci.yml). Entries are paired with their gate so a reviewer can trace 'how does this URL ever get hit?' to the chokepoint that would have to fire first.

allowed_urls.reason: npm registry — used by `npm install -g @lodestone/cli` and `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` to fetch the package itself + its declared dependencies. Never contacted by the running tool; the URL only appears in lockfiles / package metadata, never in shipped runtime code paths.
allowed_urls.gated_by: out-of-process (npm/pnpm); not a Lodestone runtime path

allowed_urls.reason: Default embedder weights (nomic-embed-text-v1.5 ONNX int8). Bundled inside @lodestone/ingest at publish time via `pnpm bundle-models`. The URL is referenced ONLY from `lodestone setup-models` (opt-in re-fetch path) and never from the steady-state runtime — the bundled copy on disk is what actually loads.
allowed_urls.gated_by: two-gate: (1) operator opt-in via `--allow-download` flag OR `LODESTONE_ALLOW_MODEL_DOWNLOAD=1`, (2) `assertNetworkAllowed('setup-models: nomic-embed-text')` which throws when LODESTONE_OFFLINE=1

allowed_urls.reason: Fallback embedder weights (snowflake-arctic-embed-s ONNX int8) — used when the friend explicitly sets `[embedder].profile = 'tiny'` in lodestone.toml. Bundled at publish time alongside the default; URL only fires if the bundled copy is missing AND the friend has opted into download.
allowed_urls.gated_by: two-gate: (1) operator opt-in via `--allow-download` flag OR `LODESTONE_ALLOW_MODEL_DOWNLOAD=1`, (2) `assertNetworkAllowed('snowflake fallback weights')` which throws when LODESTONE_OFFLINE=1
allowed_urls.scope: setup-models opt-in re-fetch + tiny-profile cold-start fallback

allowed_urls.reason: Reserved for future direct-from-maintainer fetches of the nomic-ai org's bundled assets. Currently UNUSED in code but pre-approved here so adding a nomic-ai variant to the §05 manifest does not require a separate manifest change. If this ever appears in dist/ without a corresponding setup-models entry it is a bug.
allowed_urls.gated_by: two-gate (same as Xenova/Snowflake): operator opt-in + assertNetworkAllowed

allowed_urls.reason: Reserved for the future code-aware embedder swap (ibm-granite/granite-embedding-english-r2 — the §05 deep-plan §10 768-dim fallback once the code-aware tier ships). Currently UNUSED in code; pre-approved so the §10 follow-on does not have to re-amend this manifest.
allowed_urls.gated_by: two-gate (same as Xenova/Snowflake): operator opt-in + assertNetworkAllowed

allowed_urls.reason: SPDX license identifier root — appears in source-header comments that survive into compiled output for some build settings. Inert at runtime; never the target of an outbound HTTP call. Allowlisted in the §18 audit so spurious header preservation does not trip the build.

enforcement.runtime: packages/shared/src/net/fetch.ts → assertNetworkAllowed() throws NetworkBlockedError when LODESTONE_OFFLINE=1; every fetch in the codebase routes through this gate
enforcement.ci_workflow: .github/workflows/ci.yml → `privacy-audit` job runs the no-outbound-urls vitest suite against compiled dist/ and fails the build on any unexpected URL
